input.command:
/usr/bin/bash -lc "python - <<'PY'
import json,re
fn='articles/2026/03/25/articles.jsonl'
queries={
 'q489_uruguay':['Uruguay','Wembley'],
 'q489_england':['England','defender'],
 'q659_footballer':['Israeli Premier League'],
 'q796_houses':['residential houses'],
 'q320_bank':['Paris bank'],
 'q320_telegram':['Bank of America','Telegram'],
 'q795_journos':['Lebanese journalists'],
 'q267_cutoff':['thousands cut off'],
 'q177_city':['southern Israeli city'],
 'q177_arad':['Arad','Houthis'],
 'q560_adnoc':['ADNOC','Abu Dhabi'],
 'q144_worship':['all religions','Jerusalem'],
 'q263_water':['fresh water'],
}

def sf(a,k): return a.get(k) or ''
def snip(txt,terms):
    low=txt.lower(); idx=[low.find(t.lower()) for t in terms if low.find(t.lower())>=0]
    i=min(idx) if idx else 0
    return re.sub(r'\\s+',' ',txt[max(0,i-250):i+900])
hits={k:[] for k in queries}
for line in open(fn,encoding='utf-8'):
    if not line.strip(): continue
    a=json.loads(line); txt=sf(a,'title')+'\\n'+sf(a,'content'); low=txt.lower()
    for k,terms in queries.items():
        if all(t.lower() in low for t in terms):
            hits[k].append((sf(a,'date_publish') or sf(a,'date'),sf(a,'source'),sf(a,'title'),snip(txt,terms)))
for k,arr in hits.items():
    print('\\n###',k,'hits',len(arr))
    seen=set(); c=0
    for d,src,title,s in arr[:8]:
        key=(src,title)
        if key in seen: continue
        seen.add(key); c+=1
        print(f'[{d}] {src} | {title}')
        print(s[:1000])
        if c>=4: break
PY"

import json,re
fn='articles/2026/03/25/articles.jsonl'
queries={
 'q489_uruguay':['Uruguay','Wembley'],
 'q489_england':['England','defender'],
 'q659_footballer':['Israeli Premier League'],
 'q796_houses':['residential houses'],
 'q320_bank':['Paris bank'],
 'q320_telegram':['Bank of America','Telegram'],
 'q795_journos':['Lebanese journalists'],
 'q267_cutoff':['thousands cut off'],
 'q177_city':['southern Israeli city'],
 'q177_arad':['Arad','Houthis'],
 'q560_adnoc':['ADNOC','Abu Dhabi'],
 'q144_worship':['all religions','Jerusalem'],
 'q263_water':['fresh water'],
}

def sf(a,k): return a.get(k) or ''
def snip(txt,terms):
    low=txt.lower(); idx=[low.find(t.lower()) for t in terms if low.find(t.lower())>=0]
    i=min(idx) if idx else 0
    return re.sub(r'\\s+',' ',txt[max(0,i-250):i+900])
hits={k:[] for k in queries}
for line in open(fn,encoding='utf-8'):
    if not line.strip(): continue
    a=json.loads(line); txt=sf(a,'title')+'\\n'+sf(a,'content'); low=txt.lower()
    for k,terms in queries.items():
        if all(t.lower() in low for t in terms):
            hits[k].append((sf(a,'date_publish') or sf(a,'date'),sf(a,'source'),sf(a,'title'),snip(txt,terms)))
for k,arr in hits.items():
    print('\\n###',k,'hits',len(arr))
    seen=set(); c=0
    for d,src,title,s in arr[:8]:
        key=(src,title)
        if key in seen: continue
        seen.add(key); c+=1
        print(f'[{d}] {src} | {title}')
        print(s[:1000])
        if c>=4: break
PY"

Man sentenced for racist abuse of England defender Carter March 25 - A man who sent racially abusive messages to England defender Jess Carter during the women's European Championship last year has been sentenced to six weeks in prison, suspended for 12 months. The man pleaded guilty to sending a racially abusive message to Carter on social media during the tournament. He was also handed a four-year football banning order, 10 days of community work, and given a three-month curfew. "This case should be a stark reminder to so-called keyboard warriors: online actions have consequences," John Moran for the Crown Prosecution Service said. "This type of social media offending will not be tolerated. Those who peddle hate, on or off the pitch, will be brought to justice." [2026-03-25] www.straitstimes.com | Tomiyasu ruled out of Japan squad for games against Scotland, England
Tomiyasu ruled out of Japan squad for games against Scotland, England March 25 - Takehiro Tomiyasu has been ruled out of Japan's friendlies against Scotland and England due to injury, denting the Ajax Amsterdam defender's hopes of making it into the squad for this year's World Cup in North America. Tomiyasu, 27, was called up by Japan head coach Hajime Moriyasu last week, despite having not featured for the national team since 2024. The defender left Premier League side Arsenal last year after playing only six minutes in the 2024-25 season due to a knee injury. His injury issues have continued since his move to Ajax, limiting him to just six appearances in all competitions. Japan face Scotland at Hampden Park on Saturday, before taking on England at Wembley on March 31. The matches are a key part of their preparations for the World Cup, where they are in Group F with the Netherlands, Tunisia and a playoff winner. REUTERS

[2026-03-24] www.hindustantimes.com | Villagers capture leopard in Dudhwa buffer zone
. Dudhwa buffer zone getting conflict prone With frequent movements of big cats near human settlements, the Dudhwa buffer zone has kept the forest officials busy this year. On two occasions, first on Mar 16 and again on Mar 17, a leopard barged into residential houses, causing minor injuries to three persons in Rannagar and Banbirpur villages respectively before being tranquilized and rescued on Mar 17 from Banbirpur house. Dudhwa buffer zone forest teams have been patrolling regularly in the conflict areas to mitigate human-animal conflicts. Eight leopards and one tiger have been captured by buffer zone forest officials since Feb 2. However, movements of big cats are still reported from various parts. Field director, Dudhwa Tiger Reserve (DTR) Dr H Rajamohan told HT that after the harvesting of cane crops, the big cats get no safe hiding spot and they seek shelter thus causing conflict.
